content:
AUDIENCE:
This reader is a curious 8-year-old child who enjoys stories that are exciting but not too scary. They have a basic understanding of time and watches but might find complicated explanations confusing. They care about characters and what happens to them, especially when it involves adventure or mystery. They might get bored if the story is too slow or uses big, hard words. A tone that is clear, imaginative, and a little suspenseful but not frightening will work best.

===BEGIN===
One sunny morning, you look at your watch. It says 10:34! Just ten seconds later, BOOM! You think your plane exploded over the big, blue ocean! Your heart pounds fast as you close your eyes tight.

Suddenly, you wake up with a jolt! You hear the plane’s engine humming—just like before. You blink and check your watch again. This time, it says 9:35. Wait, how is that possible? You’re on the plane, flying safely, but time is all mixed up!

Maybe this is a time trick, or a secret adventure that you’re about to have. What happens next? You take a deep breath and get ready to find out, because flying through time sounds like the coolest trip ever!
===END===
